ip_outcome_note: Card name 'Mareep' on the printed card matches National Pokedex #179, Wool Pokemon, Electric-type, introduced in Generation II / Johto. Species identity is named on the card face itself. Pokemon-internal IP, no external-crossover risk, so no ## IP block is required, but ip_verified flips to true.

trivia_bullets.text: Chilling Reign is the sixth main Sword & Shield Series expansion, released June 18, 2021 — the largest SwSh set up to that point at 198 base-set cards (233 with secret rares). It's the TCG tie-in to the Crown Tundra DLC, headlined by Calyrex's TCG debut in both Ice Rider and Shadow Rider VMAX forms alongside Galarian Articuno / Zapdos / Moltres. [Bulbapedia: Chilling Reign (TCG), via WebSearch snippet] [PokeBeach: 'Chilling Reign To Be Largest SWSH Set So Far at 198 Cards']

Mareep is the 'Wool Pokemon,' National Pokedex #179, an Electric-type basic introduced in Generation II (Gold/Silver, Johto region, 1999/2000). Evolves into Flaaffy at level 15 and then into Ampharos at level 30. The line's Electric typing is flavored as static charge generated by their fleece rubbing together. [Bulbapedia: Mareep (Pokemon), via WebSearch snippet]

Printed flavor text on this card: 'Rubbing its fleece generates electricity. You'll want to pet it because it's cute, but if you use your bare hand, you'll get a painful shock.' Continuous with the Johto-era Pokedex framing — Gold's entry reads 'If static electricity builds in its body, its fleece doubles in volume. Touching it will shock you,' Silver's reads 'Its fleece grows continually. In the summer, the fleece is fully shed, but it grows back in a week.' [PokemonTCG.io: swsh6-47 flavorText] [Bulbapedia: Mareep Pokedex entries, via WebSearch snippet]

Card art is by Miki Tanaka, a Pokemon TCG illustrator since 1997 whose first card was the Fossil-set Slowpoke; she has illustrated ~238 cards across the TCG and is known for a flat, minimal-shading style with little outlined linework. [Bulbapedia: Miki Tanaka, via WebSearch snippet] [The Art of Pokemon: Miki Tanaka illustrator page]

Mareep's first TCG appearance was Neo Genesis #65/111 in 2000, the same set that introduced most of the Johto Pokemon to the card game. Chilling Reign 047/198 is a much later reprint of the species; reverse-holo variants of this print are the more collected version (market ~$0.30 reverse holo vs ~$0.11 normal as of pricing snapshot). [TCGPlayer: Mareep Neo Genesis, via WebSearch snippet] [PokemonTCG.io: swsh6-47 tcgplayer prices]

related_cards.name: Flaaffy
related_cards.note: Direct evolution from Mareep at level 15; the Mareep-Flaaffy-Ampharos line is one of Johto's most-recognizable Electric chains.

related_cards.name: Ampharos
related_cards.note: Final evolution of the line; lighthouse-tail beacon flavor makes it the marquee Pokemon of the chain in lore and in fan-art.

related_cards.name: Calyrex VMAX (Ice Rider)
related_cards.note: Set headliner — defines the Chilling Reign expansion's identity. Any narrative framing of the set's cards passes through Calyrex.

related_cards.name: Calyrex VMAX (Shadow Rider)
related_cards.note: Other set headliner; the dual-Calyrex framing is the set's marketing anchor.

related_cards.name: Mareep
related_cards.note: Species' first TCG print; vintage Johto-era touchstone for any Mareep-line bundle.
